explanation: This LTL property states that globally, if the system is in PSV mode (bool_exp1) and a sudden drop in pressure exceeding the inhale trigger sensitivity occurs (bool_exp2), then a breathing cycle shall be initiated in the next state (bool_exp3). This captures the causal relationship and the mode-specific condition mentioned in the natural language.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) mode and a sudden drop in pressure (above the user-settable Inhale Trigger Sensitivity) is detected, then a breathing cycle shall be initiated in the very next state. This captures the causal relationship where the specified pressure drop event, while in PSV mode, acts as the trigger for initiating the breathing cycle.

The property states that globally, whenever a sudden drop in pressure above the user-settable inhale trigger sensitivity for PSV mode is detected (dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV), a breathing cycle shall be initiated in the very next state (breathingCycleStart). This directly captures the reactive nature of the breathing cycle being initiated by the specified pressure drop in PSV mode, as implied by the phrase 'shall be initiated by'. The atomic proposition 'dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV' implicitly includes the 'In PSV mode' context as per its description.

The property states that globally, if the system is in Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) mode and a sudden drop in airway pressure (PAW) is detected that is greater than the user-settable Inhale Trigger Sensitivity (ITS_PSV), then the breathing cycle shall start in the very next state. This captures the requirement that the breathing cycle is initiated by this specific pressure drop while operating in PSV mode.

The property states that whenever the system is in PSV mode and a sudden drop in pressure, significant enough to meet the Inhale Trigger Sensitivity (ITS_PSV), occurs, then a breathing cycle shall be initiated. This maps to a global implication: G ((PSVMode & dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV) -> breathingCycleStart).

The property states that whenever the system is in PSV mode AND a sudden drop in pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity in PSV mode (ITS_PSV) is detected, then in the next state, the breathing cycle must start. This directly captures 'In PSV mode the breathing cycle shall be initiated by a sudden drop in pressure below a user-settable threshold (Inhale Trigger Sensitivity)'. The 'initiated by' strongly suggests a next-state temporal relationship.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is operating in PSV mode and detects a sudden drop in airway pressure that exceeds the user-settable inhale trigger sensitivity, the breathing cycle must start in the very next state. The 'X' (next) operator is used to signify that the initiation of the breathing cycle immediately follows the detection of the pressure drop.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) mode and a sudden drop in pressure occurs that is greater than the user-settable inhale trigger sensitivity, then a breathing cycle shall be initiated. This captures the requirement for breathing cycle initiation in PSV mode by the specified pressure drop trigger.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PSV mode and a sudden drop in pressure exceeding the inhale trigger sensitivity occurs, then a breathing cycle shall start in the very next state. This captures 'In PSV mode the breathing cycle shall be initiated by a sudden drop in pressure below a user-settable threshold (Inhale Trigger Sensitivity)' by interpreting the 'sudden drop in pressure below a user-settable threshold (Inhale Trigger Sensitivity)' as the condition captured by 'dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV', indicating the magnitude of the pressure drop is greater than a user-set sensitivity, which in turn triggers the breathing cycle. The 'X' operator ensures immediate initiation in the next state.

The property states that globally, whenever a sudden drop in pressure that exceeds the user-settable inhale trigger sensitivity is detected (indicated by 'dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV', which by its definition implies the system is in PSV mode), a breathing cycle must initiate in the very next state ('X breathingCycleStart'). This captures the requirement that the breathing cycle is 'initiated by' the specified pressure drop.
